title: god i love easter eggs
wikitext:
[]: god i love easter eggs Post by chezzmasterx » Sat Dec 12, 2009 1:55 pm so i was watching transformers 2 yesterday, and there is a portion in the movie where optimus prime is fighting multiple decepticons in a forest. in one scene, he has one of them in a head lock(i couldnt find this pic!). so if this hasnt already been a topic, look at it the way i see it: Michael bay directs transformers. who is to say he wouldnt want his hands on Eva? rumors have sprung up about multiple directors wanting to work with eva, but how much can really be confirmed? any ways there was a striking resemblence between the optimus fight scene and Asuka's final fight(this scene has been shouted out too many times, eg. Rideback ) so here are some pics opinions?

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Dec 12, 2009 1:58 pm Michael bay directs transformers. who is to say he wouldnt want his hands on Eva?

[]: Post by chezzmasterx » Sat Dec 12, 2009 2:09 pm if the movie is going to be americanized, michael bay is the man for the job. not supporting it, just saying, hes suitable for their interests

[]: Post by Ironfoot » Sat Dec 12, 2009 2:10 pm Unfortunately, that's not much of a comparison. I don't see any leg getting cut off in the Transformers shot, plus he's holding two weapons there instead of one. Plus, Michael Bay already said that he's going to quit making action movies, so there's very little chance that he'd direct LAEM. And that's if the movie or trilogy were to be made at all, which in itself is highly unlikely considering what has happened to ADV. Sorry to soil your spirits.

[]: Post by Legendary » Sat Dec 12, 2009 2:16 pm chezzmasterx wrote: if the movie is going to be americanized, michael bay is the man for the job. not supporting it, just saying, hes suitable for their interests

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Dec 12, 2009 3:00 pm Other that two robots fighting, they're no similarities. And if you actually see direct similarities with the two fights I'm sure it's purely coincidental.

[]: Post by chezzmasterx » Sat Dec 12, 2009 5:10 pm direct similarities: the setting: forest, foggy, war torn the build up: optimus primes last stand the odds: too many to beat colors: this is just coincidence indirect similarities: adaptation: transformers from Japan, well recieved in america media: comics/anime/manga differences: 2 swords instead of 1 2 hander [Edit*] its a stretch but most easter eggs are

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Dec 12, 2009 5:14 pm Like I said it's pure coincidence I don't think Bay(and/or the writers) was paying any homages to the EoE fight and I don't think he's even seen EVA. Plus Optimus has had "last stands" in other continuities and having a lot of opponents to beat isn't uncommon in most action flicks.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Dec 12, 2009 5:18 pm I doubt they have anything in common other then being really kickass.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Sat Dec 12, 2009 9:13 pm I don't think Evangelion had enough explosions to satisfy Michael Bay's tastes.

[]: Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sun Dec 13, 2009 12:17 am You guys are forgetting that NGE invented bipedal robots. This is either an homage or a straight-up ripoff.

[]: Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Sun Dec 13, 2009 1:32 am No. Worst thread ever.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Dec 13, 2009 2:31 am ^what he, Brik, and esselfortium said Legendary wrote: He's made a Victoria's Secret ad

[]: Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sun Dec 13, 2009 3:52 am no Michael Bay better not get his dirty damn hands on Evangelion he better not...or there will be hell to pay

[]: Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Dec 13, 2009 3:18 pm I wouldn't wish Michael Bay's ADHD hack touch on anything left in the universe, even those things I abhor.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Dec 13, 2009 5:20 pm Whether or not Bay may direct a possible Live Action Eva Movie it would still most likely suck. It's nothing to get all butthurt over.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Dec 13, 2009 11:56 pm chezzmasterx wrote: if the movie is going to be americanized, michael bay is the man for the job. not supporting it, just saying, hes suitable for their interests

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 14, 2009 1:49 am Well, I think that about does it for this thread.
